steps.title: Identify the caveat that raises doubt
steps.content: After reporting ash deposits consistent with massive eruptions, the passage states that “erosion and past resurfacing events could have modified the surface.” This caveat directly creates uncertainty about the reliability of the current evidence.

steps.title: Apply the caveat to the logical conclusion
steps.content: Choice A captures the logical consequence: when surface evidence could have been modified, current observations might not represent actual conditions. The other choices either contradict the evidence or introduce points not discussed in the passage. That matches choice A — A is correct.
